title: From The Whole To A Part
study: A Study In The Life Of John The
scriptureForWeek: MATTHEW 11:7-15 As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began talking about him to the crowds. “What kind of man did you go into the wilderness to see? Was he a weak reed, swayed by every breath of wind? Or were you expecting to see a man dressed in expensive clothes? No, people with expensive clothes live in palaces. Were you looking for a prophet? Yes, and he is more than a prophet. John is the man to whom the Scriptures refer when they say, ‘Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, and he will prepare your way before you.’ “I tell you the truth, of all who have ever lived, none is greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least person in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he is! And from the time John the Baptist began preaching until now, the Kingdom of Heaven has been forcefully advancing, and violent people are attacking it. For before John came, all the prophets and the law of Moses looked forward to this present time. And if you are willing to accept what I say, he is Elijah, the one the prophets said would come. Anyone with ears to hear should listen and understand!
weekTheme: Boys Look Inward; Men Look Up God will remember you Better than John the Baptist Well, young man, major congratulations are in order for you, because this week is the last week of shift 3: from The Whole to the Part. This may be the most challenging of all the shifts because it requires holding 2 ideas in your mind at the same time. Idea 1. You matter, and your life should be full of significance. Idea 2. You are not the center of the story, so walk in humility. Understanding how to play your part in the story of God without thinking the whole story is about you is a mature way to look at life. And it’s a realistic one. You are made in the image of God, and he has a purpose and calling for you. But there are also 8 billion other people in the world who also matter to God and have a calling and a purpose too. You have a part in the story, so play it well, but you are not the center of the story. There is only one man who is the star of the show and is worthy of being at the center. And that’s Jesus. Only he lived the perfect life, died a sacrificial death, and rose again to rule and reign forever. He is the center of human history even now, 2000 years after his death. John the Baptist knew that Jesus was the Messiah and the center of history, so he used his gifts and platform to point beyond himself to Jesus. The older you get, the more you are going to realize how important it is to take on the mindset and mentality of John the Baptist. He

days.title: What Kind Of Man Do You Want To Be?
days.scripture: MATTHEW 11:7-15 As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began talking about him to the crowds. “What kind of man did you go into the wilderness to see? Was he a weak reed, swayed by every breath of wind? Or were you expecting to see a man dressed in expensive clothes? No, people with expensive clothes live in palaces. Were you looking for a prophet? Yes, and he is more than a prophet. John is the man to whom the Scriptures refer when they say, ‘Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, and he will prepare your way before you.’ “I tell you the truth, of all who have ever lived, none is greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least person in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he is! And from the time John the Baptist began preaching until now, the Kingdom of Heaven has been forcefully advancing, and violent people are attacking it. For before John came, all the prophets and the law of Moses looked forward to this present time. And if you are willing to accept what I say, he is Elijah, the one the prophets said would come. Anyone with ears to hear should listen and understand!"
days.reflection: For the last 10 weeks, you have been looking at the life of John. You haven’t been doing all this reading and discussing for nothing. You haven’t been doing this simply so you can know the bible more or understand biblical history. You have been doing this so you can know how to become the right kind of man. In the passage today, Jesus asked the crowds a question. It’s a question you have to ask yourself. It’s a question the world is going to ask of you. What kind of man do you want to be? If you don’t get a vision of the kind of man you want to be, you will unintentionally become any sort of man. It’s important to get a vision of this because there are different kinds of men in the world today. There are foolish men. They don’t fear God, they don’t learn how life works, and they drift along and never learn from their mistakes. There are godless men. They live however they want and delude themselves into thinking that there are no real consequences for rebellion against God. There are immature men. They are the biological age of adults but have the mindset of teenagers. There are selfish men. They only live for themselves and take from those around them. There are weak men. They are pushed around and easily manipulated by others. They don’t stand up for themselves, others, or their beliefs. But… There are godly men. They love and fear God and enjoy his presence and favor in their lives. There are wise men. They seek to understand how to live skillfully and deal with the challenges of life. There are strong men. They have backbone and courage, and they stick to their convictions regardless of what other people think. There are kind men. They are gracious, considerate, and merciful to others. There are brave men. They are willing to do what most other men are afraid of. Jesus wanted the crowds to know the kind of man that John the Baptist was. It's clear that Jesus had tons of respect for John because of his faithfulness and friendship. John was set apart in his generation and Jesus wanted to honor his legacy in front of others. Paul told Timothy to look for the right kind of men to help him lead his church. 2 Timothy 2:1-2 says, “Timothy, my dear son, be strong through the grace that God gives you in Christ Jesus. You have heard me teach things that have been confirmed by many reliable witnesses. Now teach these truths to other trustworthy people who will be able to pass them on to others.” Jesus spent his time disciplining teenage guys to help them become the right kind of men. Paul urged Timothy to find the right kind of men. Your generation is waiting for the right kind of men. What sort of man do you want to be when you get older? What do you want to be said about you? How do you want Jesus to answer that question? Why don’t you spend some time on that question; it could really shape and affect what you do with your teenage years.
questions: If you were to describe the kind of man you want to be in 4 or 5 words, what would they be?
What sort of values do guys your age tend to think are important in a person's life? Do you think these are biblical and helpful? Why or why not?
Ask the person you are doing this study with to share what he wants to be said about the kind of man he was at the end of his life. Why?

thoughts.verse: As the time of King David’s death approached, he gave this charge to his son Solomon: “I am going where everyone on earth must someday go. Take courage and be a man. Observe the requirements of the LORD your God, and follow all his ways. Keep the decrees, commands, regulations, and laws written in the Law of Moses so that you will be successful in all you do and wherever you go.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to fill your heart with vision and passion to be a godly, wise, and courageous man.

days.title: No More Swaying In The Breeze
days.scripture: MATTHEW 11:7-15 As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began talking about him to the crowds. “What kind of man did you go into the wilderness to see? Was he a weak reed, swayed by every breath of wind? Or were you expecting to see a man dressed in expensive clothes? No, people with expensive clothes live in palaces. Were you looking for a prophet? Yes, and he is more than a prophet. John is the man to whom the Scriptures refer when they say, ‘Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, and he will prepare your way before you.’ “I tell you the truth, of all who have ever lived, none is greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least person in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he is! And from the time John the Baptist began preaching until now, the Kingdom of Heaven has been forcefully advancing, and violent people are attacking it. For before John came, all the prophets and the law of Moses looked forward to this present time. And if you are willing to accept what I say, he is Elijah, the one the prophets said would come. Anyone with ears to hear should listen and understand!"
days.reflection: You do not want to be a pushover. You also don’t want to be a jerk. You want to be something else. You want to be a man who holds his ground. John the Baptist knew how to confront without condemning and how to live with conviction without compromise. He was bold and strong, yet loving and loyal. Jesus gave him a great compliment, one that you want to be said about you: “He was not a weak read, easily swayed by the wind.” One Bible commentator explains why Jesus used this example. “The reed to which Jesus referred was common along Near Eastern river banks, including those of the Jordan where John baptized. They were light and flexible, waving back and forth with every breeze. The people knew that John was not swayed like those reeds. If ever there had been a man with unswerving convictions, it was John. He stood up to the scribes, the Pharisees, the Sadducees, and even to Herod himself—for which boldness he was now in prison. The people knew John was as far as possible from being spineless or irresolute.” John could have softened his message to make people like him. He could have overlooked the leaders’ sins and avoided trouble by just pointing out the problems of the people. But he didn’t. He called the religious leaders a brood of vipers and warned them of God's wrath. He told the Roman Soldiers not to be exploitative. He told the people to open their hearts because God was on the move. John was a man of both conviction and compassion. John was a man who wasn’t pushed around by the powerful or the systems of his day. He wasn’t intimidated by other people’s opinions of him because he understood his calling. Pleasing God was more important to him than fitting in. James 1:6-8 says, “Do not waver, for a person with divided loyalty is as unsettled as a wave of the sea that is blown and tossed by the wind. Such people should not expect to receive anything from the Lord. Their loyalty is divided between God and the world, and they are unstable in everything they do.” Blown and tossed around. Divided loyalty, unstable. You do not want to live like this. You want to be rooted, grounded, strong, and stable. This is how you will play your part in the story of God well. God will give you the courage if you learn to walk with him, care about what he wants from you, and stand firm with the power he provides. And think about how Jesus took courage and conviction to another level. Jesus challenged the religious leaders. He welcomed those who were outcasts and didn’t care what people thought. He wrestled with his father in prayer and then resolved to go to the cross. He forgave his enemies and died for our sins. Then he rose again and defeated death. To become like Jesus and follow him well isn’t just about avoiding sin, or trying to be “nice” or “good.” It's about becoming a man of conviction and courage, one who fulfills the mission God has for him with boldness and love. As you go through life, you will be tempted to be swayed by the opinions of your friends and the views of the world. You will feel the pressure to conform and compromise. Don’t give in, and don’t be a self-righteous jerk. Hold your ground with conviction and compassion. You will have the affirmation and acceptance you really long for. The one that comes from God.
questions: How do you feel when you experience peer pressure? Fear, shame, anxiety?
Have you ever given in to peer pressure for the sake of avoiding conflict, fear of being left out, or the desire to fit in? Would you be willing to share about it?

Ask the person you are doing this study with about a moment they gave in to peer pressure. What was it, and what happened? Then ask them about a moment when they resisted it and held their ground. What happened?

thoughts.verse: Then we will no longer be immature like children. We won’t be tossed and blown about by every wind of new teaching. We will not be influenced when people try to trick us with lies so clever they sound like the truth. Instead, we will speak the truth in love, growing in every way more and more like Christ, who is the head of his body, the church.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you the strength and courage to be a man of conviction. Someone who can stand their ground and not give in to the opinions and whims of the crowd.

days.title: Servants Not Celebrities
days.scripture: MATTHEW 11:7-15 As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began talking about him to the crowds. “What kind of man did you go into the wilderness to see? Was he a weak reed, swayed by every breath of wind? Or were you expecting to see a man dressed in expensive clothes? No, people with expensive clothes live in palaces. Were you looking for a prophet? Yes, and he is more than a prophet. John is the man to whom the Scriptures refer when they say, ‘Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, and he will prepare your way before you.’ “I tell you the truth, of all who have ever lived, none is greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least person in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he is! And from the time John the Baptist began preaching until now, the Kingdom of Heaven has been forcefully advancing, and violent people are attacking it. For before John came, all the prophets and the law of Moses looked forward to this present time. And if you are willing to accept what I say, he is Elijah, the one the prophets said would come. Anyone with ears to hear should listen and understand!"
days.reflection: People love celebrities. It’s actually kind of crazy. People pay attention to every detail of their lives. People care what brands they wear, what cars they drive, what they post on social media, and who they date. They are almost treated like demi-gods. They are special, sensational, and worthy of our attention. And celebrities seem to revel in this. They get awesome treatment, tons of perks, and are at the center of our world’s attention. Wanting to become a celebrity and be famous has taken over our world. You need to guard your heart against this because it will distort the kind of man become. Author David Brooks points out that “fame used to rank low as a life’s ambition for most people.” In a 1976 survey that asked people to list their life goals, fame ranked fifteenth out of sixteen. By 2007, 51 percent of young people reported that being famous was one of their top personal goals. Now according to CBS news. 86 percent of teens hope to become social media influencers. In one study, middle school girls were asked who they would most like to have dinner with. Jennifer Lopez came in first, Jesus Christ came in second, and Paris Hilton third. The girls were then asked which of the following jobs they would like to have. Nearly twice as many said they’d rather be a celebrity’s personal assistant (for example, Justin Bieber’s) than the president of Harvard. Crowds love to gather around celebrities, and celebrities love crowds. But Jesus points out something interesting about John the Baptist. He wasn’t a celebrity; he was a servant. He wanted to help the people by telling them the truth not hijack their thinking for himself. Jesus said, Or were you expecting to see a man dressed in expensive clothes? No, people with expensive clothes live in palaces. John didn’t want to be the center of attention, but he knew how to handle the attention. He didn’t get sucked up into the hype and let it go to his head. He had a mission, and the mission was about setting the stage for Jesus. He got the attention, but he didn’t keep the attention. His call was to pass it on to Christ. Celebrities want all eyes on them; John wanted all eyes on Jesus. Narcissists want everyone to fix their gaze on them; John wanted people to lift their eyes and behold the lamb of God who took away the sins of the world. As you grow up and move through life, you never know what will happen to you. You may be successful; you may find yourself in the spotlight. If that does happen, don’t let it get to your head. Stay humble, don’t get sucked into the hype, and stay on mission. John did decrease so that Jesus could increase, and that should be the vision of every man—even you. We are called to be servants, not celebrities. We are called to be faithful, not famous. Just handle your business in a godly way, live with integrity, work with excellence, and let God sort out the rest. Psalm 34:15 says, “The eyes of the LORD watch over those who do right; his ears are open to their cries for help.” You are seen and known and loved by God. You already have his attention, and that’s enough for any man.
questions: No shame—is there a celebrity that you like? Sports, Youtube, etc? What do you find interesting or cool about them?
Why do you think people feel like they need so much attention today?
Ask the person you are doing this study with how they handle attention when they get it. What do they do to avoid becoming a narcissist and thinking it’s all about them?

thoughts.verse: “Watch out! Don’t do your good deeds publicly, to be admired by others, for you will lose the reward from your Father in heaven. When you give to someone in need, don’t do as the hypocrites do—blowing trumpets in the synagogues and streets to call attention to their acts of charity! I tell you the truth, they have received all the reward they will ever get. But when you give to someone in need, don’t let your left hand know what your right hand is doing. Give your gifts in private, and your Father, who sees everything, will reward you."
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to break the addiction to attention in your heart. Ask him for contentment in being known and loved by God.

days.title: P Rophets Not Entertainers
days.scripture: MATTHEW 11:7-15 As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began talking about him to the crowds. “What kind of man did you go into the wilderness to see? Was he a weak reed, swayed by every breath of wind? Or were you expecting to see a man dressed in expensive clothes? No, people with expensive clothes live in palaces. Were you looking for a prophet? Yes, and he is more than a prophet. John is the man to whom the Scriptures refer when they say, ‘Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, and he will prepare your way before you.’ “I tell you the truth, of all who have ever lived, none is greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least person in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he is! And from the time John the Baptist began preaching until now, the Kingdom of Heaven has been forcefully advancing, and violent people are attacking it. For before John came, all the prophets and the law of Moses looked forward to this present time. And if you are willing to accept what I say, he is Elijah, the one the prophets said would come. Anyone with ears to hear should listen and understand!"
days.reflection: One of the greatest mistakes men make as they go through life is thinking that the purpose of life is to keep everyone happy. But that is just impossible. The only people who work to make everyone happy are clowns. Don’t be a clown. Be a man. You may have heard the quote, “To escape criticism, do nothing, say nothing, be nothing.” But you can't go through your life like that. God has called you to say things, do things, become something. You are called to be a witness to the truth. You are called to become a godly man. You are called to a mission and a purpose. And that’s where it gets hard. John the Baptist was a prophet, not an entertainer. He wasn’t there to make everyone smile; he was there to bring the truth. Jesus said this about JTB: Were you looking for a prophet? Yes, and he is more than a prophet. John is the man to whom the Scriptures refer when they say, ‘Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, and he will prepare your way before you.’ It’s really important for a guy your age to remember that you will experience resistance and criticism if you live for Jesus. There will be people who don’t like your message, call you judgmental, mock what you believe, and tell you to shut up. Don’t let that beat you down or silence you. Don’t let it discourage you or make you comply with their demands. God has not called you to be an entertainer. You were not put on earth to make people smile. If you want to make people smile, get a job at an ice cream shop. If you want to be a man that makes a difference, embrace your calling as a forerunner and a prophet. The world is longing for guys your age to grow up into men who will tell the truth. Men willing to go against what is popular and live for what is right. Men who can handle the boos of the crowd for the applause of heaven. Men who bear witness to the gospel, and don’t shrink back in fear. Even though John the Baptist's life was short, it was potent and prophetic. He is remembered and respected for his commitment to the truth and devotion to the mission. Loving someone means telling them what they need to hear in a way they can receive it, not avoiding the truth for fear of rejection. Jesus honored John as a man of conviction and truth. And he will honor you too if you hold fast to the truth.
questions: Who is the most courageous person you know when it comes to speaking the truth? Why?

What is the hardest thing about having someone reject you? Is there anyone in your life that doesn’t like you right now, that is stressing you out or beating you down? Would you be willing to share about it?

Ask the person doing this study with you to tell you about a time they faced rejection when they were your age. How did they handle it, work through it, or get over it? What advice do they have for you at this age?

thoughts.verse: “Everyone who acknowledges me publicly here on earth, I will also acknowledge before my Father in heaven. But everyone who denies me here on earth, I will also deny before my Father in heaven. Don’t imagine that I came to bring peace to the earth! I came not to bring peace, but a sword."
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you from it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to strengthen your heart in any areas you are feeling tempted to shrink back or shut up. Ask him for skills on how to handle rejection and be a winsome witness for the truth.

days.title: You’Ve Got Something Better Than John
days.scripture: MATTHEW 11:7-15 As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began talking about him to the crowds. “What kind of man did you go into the wilderness to see? Was he a weak reed, swayed by every breath of wind? Or were you expecting to see a man dressed in expensive clothes? No, people with expensive clothes live in palaces. Were you looking for a prophet? Yes, and he is more than a prophet. John is the man to whom the Scriptures refer when they say, ‘Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you, and he will prepare your way before you.’ “I tell you the truth, of all who have ever lived, none is greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least person in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he is! And from the time John the Baptist began preaching until now, the Kingdom of Heaven has been forcefully advancing, and violent people are attacking it. For before John came, all the prophets and the law of Moses looked forward to this present time. And if you are willing to accept what I say, he is Elijah, the one the prophets said would come. Anyone with ears to hear should listen and understand!"
days.reflection: It can be tempting at your age to read about men like John the Baptist and think, “I could never be like him. I don’t have his personality, his strength, or his gifts.” And it's true—John was an amazing man. In fact, Jesus said that of all the men born of women, John the Baptist was the greatest. YET… Jesus goes on to say something crazy. Something that sounds impossible. Something that you will struggle to believe. Read these words from this morning’s reading closely. “I tell you the truth, of all who have ever lived, none is greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least person in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he is!” What does this even mean? How can it be possible that the least in the kingdom is greater than JTB? It all comes down to what we have after Jesus versus what John had before Jesus. Before Jesus came, people related to God by obeying his law. He gave them commands and promises based on their obedience and loyalty. They had to live up to what God said and struggled to do so. But Jesus came to bring us into a new way of relating to God. It was called the New Covenant. It’s based on what Jesus did for us, and we receive it as a free gift by trusting in what he has done. John understood this because he said that Jesus was going to bring a better baptism and a better kingdom. And you get to be a part of that kingdom if you choose. Repenting of your sins, turning to Jesus, and putting your trust in him enables you to enter the kingdom of God and be born into his family. John 1:12-13 puts it this way: “But to all who believed him and accepted him, he gave the right to become children of God. They are reborn—not with a physical birth resulting from human passion or plan, but a birth that comes from God.” You can be born into a new family: the family of God. You get to be a son and not a slave. And the spiritual family of God is amazing. You get every blessing in the spiritual places in Christ. A new name, new identity, new future, new hope, and a new inheritance. And being born of the Spirit into the kingdom is better than being born of women, like JTB. God wants to offer you a place in his family. He wants to use you like he used John the Baptist. He wants to save you from being a narcissist who thinks that life is all about you, and save you from shame thinking that you have nothing to offer. If you let him, Jesus will help you make the shift from a whole to a part. He will give you a place in his story—the story we all long to hear. Are you in?
questions: What has stood out to you the most from the last 10 weeks on John the Baptist?
What has been inspiring, and what has been challenging?
How are you doing right now holding the tension between knowing you matter and also knowing your life isn’t all about you? Can you unpack it a little bit?
Ask the person you are doing this study with how they hold the tension between greatness and servanthood, significance and humility.
Are you ready for the most difficult, difficulty day yet?

thoughts.verse: But, When God our Savior revealed his kindness and love, he saved us, not because of the righteous things we had done, but because of his mercy. He washed away our sins, giving us a new birth and new life through the Holy Spirit. He generously poured out the Spirit upon us through Jesus Christ our Savior. Because of his grace he made us right in his sight and gave us confidence that we will inherit eternal life.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you vision to be a godly and passionate man. A man of confidence, character, and compassion. A man who leaves a strong legacy of impact and love.
